historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: H2S excursion during confined vessel entry
historical_incidents.summary: Gas detector flagged rising H2S inside a confined vessel during an active confined-space-entry permit; entrant evacuated before exposure threshold reached.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: gas pressure rise with concurrent hot work
historical_incidents.summary: Pressure anomaly in a vessel bay coincided with an active hot-work permit nearby; work was suspended after the operational risk review flagged the combination, avoiding potential ignition of accumulated gas.

historical_incidents.title: Fatal incident: entrapped gas with active hot work permit during shift handover
historical_incidents.summary: Entrapped gas in a confined vessel went undetected across a shift handover while a hot-work permit remained active; ignition occurred shortly after handover completion. Root cause review found no system correlated the gas trend with the active permit across the handover boundary.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: vibration spike during breakdown maintenance on reactor pump
historical_incidents.summary: Unplanned breakdown maintenance combined with rising pump vibration trend; early shutdown avoided seal failure.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: temperature excursion in reactor unit during electrical isolation
historical_incidents.summary: Temperature anomaly during a routine electrical isolation; isolation work paused and excursion traced to a stuck control valve, not a hazard escalation -- recorded as a low-severity correlated event.

historical_incidents.title: Recordable incident: storage tank overpressure during inspection
historical_incidents.summary: Pressure relief activated during a scheduled inspection window combined with an unusual fill-rate change; minor release, no injuries, but highlighted lack of cross-system correlation between maintenance schedule and tank telemetry.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: confined space gas anomaly with no permit active
historical_incidents.summary: Single-sensor gas spike with no active permit and no maintenance context; investigation found a sensor drift fault, not a real hazard. Cited internally as the textbook example of why single-sensor alarms must not auto-escalate without context.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: compressor house pressure and vibration co-trend during preventive maintenance
historical_incidents.summary: Preventive maintenance window coincided with a slow co-trending rise in pressure and vibration; flagged early, compressor taken offline for inspection before failure.

historical_incidents.title: Recordable incident: pipe rack leak during excavation nearby
historical_incidents.summary: Excavation permit active near a buried utility line; subsequent ground movement contributed to a small flange leak on the adjacent pipe rack, detected by pressure drop correlated with excavation activity logs.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: cooling water plant vibration with no corroborating context
historical_incidents.summary: Isolated vibration spike on a cooling water pump with no permit or maintenance activity; resolved as routine mechanical noise from a nearby vehicle pass, not flagged for action -- another reference case for the no-context-no-escalation principle.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: effluent treatment temperature rise during breakdown maintenance
historical_incidents.summary: Breakdown maintenance on a treatment pump coincided with a temperature rise; correlation flagged early, contained before any environmental exceedance.

historical_incidents.title: Fatal incident: confined space entry without verified gas-free certificate during handover
historical_incidents.summary: Entry proceeded into a confined space across a shift handover without explicit re-verification of the gas-free certificate; a slow-accumulating gas pocket caused asphyxiation. Industry root-cause analysis: handover windows require explicit re-validation of safety-critical certificates, not implicit carry-over.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: reactor B pressure-temperature co-anomaly with active hot work
historical_incidents.summary: Concurrent pressure and temperature anomalies during an active hot-work permit; work suspended and root cause traced to a faulty relief valve, narrowly avoiding a more serious event.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: storage tank farm gas reading during lifting operation
historical_incidents.summary: Elevated CH4 reading during a lifting permit near tank vents; lift paused, ventilation increased, reading normalized -- attributed to a temporary vent misalignment.

historical_incidents.title: Recordable incident: control room HVAC fault masking heat stress risk
historical_incidents.summary: Control room temperature drifted upward undetected for several hours due to a sensor calibration gap; no corroborating maintenance or permit context, but flagged retrospectively as a sensor-maintenance scheduling gap rather than a process hazard.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: manifold pressure anomaly with no active work
historical_incidents.summary: Single pressure transient on the manifold with zero permits or maintenance active; traced to a transient upstream compressor cycling event, no escalation warranted.

historical_incidents.title: Near-miss: vessel bay gas anomaly during confined space entry, handover overlap
historical_incidents.summary: Gas anomaly during an active confined-space-entry permit that overlapped a shift handover window; entry team alerted before handover completed, evacuation executed smoothly.

regulatory_guidelines.title: OISD-style guideline: concurrent hot work and confined space entry
regulatory_guidelines.excerpt_summary: Hot work permits issued for locations adjacent to active confined-space entries require an explicit joint risk assessment before either permit is authorized; the combination must not be approved as two independent permits in isolation.

regulatory_guidelines.title: OISD-style guideline: shift handover re-validation
regulatory_guidelines.excerpt_summary: Safety-critical certificates including gas-free certification and active permit status must be explicitly re-verified at every shift handover, not implicitly carried over from the outgoing shift's records.

regulatory_guidelines.title: OISD-style guideline: single-sensor alarm escalation
regulatory_guidelines.excerpt_summary: A single sensor anomaly without corroborating operational, maintenance, or permit context should not trigger a high-priority alarm; escalation criteria must require corroboration from at least one independent data source to limit alarm fatigue and preserve operator trust in the alarm system.

regulatory_guidelines.title: OISD-style guideline: breakdown maintenance risk weighting
regulatory_guidelines.excerpt_summary: Unplanned breakdown maintenance carries inherently higher risk weighting than scheduled preventive maintenance due to reduced preparation time and should be weighted accordingly in any automated risk scoring system.

regulatory_guidelines.title: OISD-style guideline: excavation near live utilities
regulatory_guidelines.excerpt_summary: Excavation permits within a defined proximity of live pressurized utility lines require continuous monitoring of adjacent line pressure for the duration of the excavation activity.
